summary: Renamed known fighter death callbacks from address-style func_* symbols to descriptive ft<Character>_OnDeath names, and updated the ft_OnDeath callback table plus C declarations/definitions for fighters already in source. The PR was a naming/cleanup pass intended to align the ft_OnDeath table with existing conventions such as ftSandbag_OnDeath and ft*_OnLoad.
what_changed: Updated the ft_OnDeath table in asm/melee/ft/code_8008521C.s from func_* entries to named callbacks including ftMario_OnDeath, ftFox_OnDeath, ftCFalcon_OnDeath, ftDonkey_OnDeath, ftKirby_OnDeath, ftKoopa_OnDeath, ftLink_OnDeath, ftSeak_OnDeath, ftNess_OnDeath, ftPeach_OnDeath, ftPopo_OnDeath, ftNana_OnDeath, ftPikachu_OnDeath, ftSamus_OnDeath, ftYoshi_OnDeath, ftPurin_OnDeath, ftMewtwo_OnDeath, ftLuigi_OnDeath, ftMars_OnDeath, ftZelda_OnDeath, ftCLink_OnDeath, ftDrMario_OnDeath, ftFalco_OnDeath, ftPichu_OnDeath, ftGameWatch_OnDeath, ftGanon_OnDeath, ftRoy_OnDeath, ftMasterhand_OnDeath, ftCrazyhand_OnDeath, ftZakoBoy_OnDeath, ftZakoGirl_OnDeath, and ftGKoopa_OnDeath.

Renamed assembly labels and their .global declarations for many character files, e.g. func_80148C64 to ftCLink_OnDeath, func_80155E18 to ftCrazyhand_OnDeath, func_8014EEF8 to ftRoy_OnDeath, func_80149ACC to ftFalco_OnDeath, func_800E2888 to ftCFalcon_OnDeath, and func_8014A250 to ftGameWatch_OnDeath.

Handled multi-callback assembly files by renaming both relevant OnDeath callbacks, including fticeclimber.s with ftPopo_OnDeath and ftNana_OnDeath, and ftkirby.s with ftKirby_OnDeath and ftDonkey_OnDeath.

Renamed C source definitions for fighters already represented in src, including func_8014949C to ftDrMario_OnDeath, func_800E5554 to ftFox_OnDeath, func_80144DFC to ftMewtwo_OnDeath, func_801147C0 to ftNess_OnDeath, and func_8011B51C to ftPeach_OnDeath.
Updated matching header declarations for the renamed C functions in ftdrmario.h, ftfox.h, ftmewtwo.h, ftness.h, and ftpeach.h.
No behavioral code changes are shown in the excerpt; the line counts are balanced at 96 additions and 96 deletions and the diffs are symbol/name replacements.
smart_moves: Renamed both producer symbols and all callback table references together, avoiding dangling labels in ft_OnDeath.
Kept the existing character prefix style instead of inventing a new naming scheme: ft<Character>_OnDeath mirrors ftSandbag_OnDeath and ft*_OnLoad.
Applied the cleanup across both assembly-only fighters and fighters already decompiled into C, preserving consistency between asm labels, C function definitions, and headers.
Used table position and existing ft_OnLoad naming context to identify the purpose of previously anonymous func_* callbacks.
Preserved exact function bodies and callback order, making this a low-risk semantic no-op while improving searchability.
decomp_lessons: Callback tables are strong evidence for naming unknown functions; when a table has a known semantic name like ft_OnDeath, each entry can often be named by table role plus character order.
When renaming a function referenced from assembly data tables, update the global label, the local label, and every .4byte table reference in the same pass.
For mixed asm/C decomp states, rename C definitions and header prototypes at the same time as assembly table symbols so future C migrations inherit the same names.
Character-specific lifecycle callbacks should use stable lifecycle suffixes like _OnLoad and _OnDeath rather than keeping address-based func_* names.
A broad symbol-only rename can be safe and useful when additions and deletions are balanced and no instructions or function bodies are changed.
naming_conventions: Use ft<Character>_OnDeath for fighter death callbacks.
Match existing lifecycle callback capitalization and suffix style, e.g. ftSandbag_OnDeath and ftCrazyhand_OnLoad.
Use project-specific internal character names where already established: ftCLink_OnDeath, ftCFalcon_OnDeath, ftGKoopa_OnDeath, ftMars_OnDeath, ftPurin_OnDeath, ftSeak_OnDeath, ftKoopa_OnDeath.
Use separate names for related character callbacks in shared files, e.g. ftPopo_OnDeath and ftNana_OnDeath in fticeclimber.s.
Use the roster/table character identity rather than the source filename when they differ, e.g. ftemblem.s contains ftRoy_OnDeath and ftkirby.s contains ftDonkey_OnDeath in the shown excerpt.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Assembly renames changed both '.global func_xxxxxxxx' and the following label to '.global ft<Character>_OnDeath' plus 'ft<Character>_OnDeath:'.
The ft_OnDeath table entries in code_8008521C.s were updated from raw func_* addresses to named symbols without changing table order.
For simple no-op or short callbacks such as ftCrazyhand_OnDeath and ftMasterhand_OnDeath, descriptive names were still applied even when the body is only a blr in the excerpt.
The tactic relies on symbol substitution only, so it should not affect matching unless unresolved symbol references are missed.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body explicitly states the rationale: all OnDeath callbacks in the ft_OnDeath table were renamed in the style of existing ftSandbag_OnDeath and ft*_OnLoad functions.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining func_* entries in fighter lifecycle callback tables such as ft_OnLoad and ft_OnDeath.
Audit whether names like ftSeak_OnDeath, ftMars_OnDeath, ftPurin_OnDeath, and ftKoopa_OnDeath remain the intended project spellings or were later normalized.
Check whether all renamed OnDeath functions have matching prototypes once their character files are migrated from asm to C.
Search for remaining references to the old func_* symbols renamed in PR 278 to verify there are no stale declarations or comments.
